entries.question: When deploying your agent to AgentSpace, how can you confirm the agent is ready for user testing?
options: A test prompt automatically launches in full-screen view.
The AgentSpace icon displays a green verification badge.
The deployment tab displays the "Draft" status.
The status changes to "Deployed" and the type shows "Online".
entries.correctAnswer: The status changes to "Deployed" and the type shows "Online".

entries.question: While setting up your project in Agent Lab, you encounter a message stating "No watsonx Runtime service detected". How should you proceed with agent configuration?
options: Create a new watsonx.ai runtime environment and associate it with the project.
Restart the Agent Lab environment to refresh service detection.
Select a different region to bypass the runtime error.
Enable the Preview mode to bypass the runtime check.
entries.correctAnswer: Create a new watsonx.ai runtime environment and associate it with the project.

entries.question: What is the main purpose of writing a detailed prompt in the Instructions tab when setting up the Personal Health Advisor agent?
options: To configure fallback options in case of tool failure.
To train the model to improve performance on fitness datasets.
To store user preferences securely for future queries.
To clearly define the agent’s tasks and how it interacts with users.
entries.correctAnswer: To clearly define the agent’s tasks and how it interacts with users.

entries.question: Why is a hosted runtime used in Google Colab during the AI agent setup?
options: To write and execute code using Google’s cloud infrastructure.
To store notebooks securely on GitHub.
To enable access to local system resources.
To block third-party libraries from being installed.
entries.correctAnswer: To write and execute code using Google’s cloud infrastructure.

entries.question: After creating a Replicate account, you’re directed to the dashboard to generate an API token. Why is this Replicate API token necessary when working in a Colab notebook?
options: To store model outputs from Replicate in your local system.
To let the notebook call Replicate models securely while running.
To export AI agent data directly to your Replicate account.
To update Replicate models from within Google Colab.
entries.correctAnswer: To let the notebook call Replicate models securely while running.

entries.question: Why is tool calling used while evaluating an AI agent’s performance?
options: It enables the agent to edit its own prompt for better accuracy.
It allows the agent to display code outputs in the notebook.
It enables the agent to use pre-defined tools to answer specific questions.
It helps the agent ask follow-up questions to the user.
entries.correctAnswer: It enables the agent to use pre-defined tools to answer specific questions.

entries.question: Why do you need a Replicate account when setting up the environment for your agent?
options: To run AI models without the need for advanced hardware.
To save the Colab notebook in the cloud.
To track version history of your Jupyter notebooks.
To enable offline access to GitHub repositories.
entries.correctAnswer: To run AI models without the need for advanced hardware.

entries.question: What aspects of the agent's responses are evaluated using MLflow?
options: Response time and storage usage
User engagement metrics and API latency
Accuracy, clarity, and relevance of final responses
Model training metrics and system resource usage
entries.correctAnswer: Accuracy, clarity, and relevance of final responses

entries.question: You enable tools such as Google Search and WebCrawler in your agent setup. What key functionality do these tools provide for the Personal Health Advisor agent?
options: Allow access to APIs for fitness tracking devices.
Enable voice-based interaction with external assistants.
Allow real-time access to context, such as weather or user location.
Enable auto-correction for inaccurate user entries.
entries.correctAnswer: Allow real-time access to context, such as weather or user location.

entries.question: In the model configuration step, you select the llama-3-3-70b-instruct model for your Personal Health Advisor agent. Why do you select this model?
options: The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model supports multilingual summarization and rapid indexing.
